title: A Model of Artificial Emotions for Behavior-modulation and Implicit Coordination in Multi-robot Systems
booktitle: Proceedings of the Genetic and Evolutionary Computation Conference
description: Similar to natural emotions in animals, artificial emotions in robots play two roles: they modulate individual behavior, and serve as tools for social interaction. In this contribution, we apply artificial emotions to a multi-robot system.
author: Guzzi, Jérôme and Giusti, Alessandro and Gambardella, Luca M. and Di Caro, Gianni A.

title: An Integer Linear Programming Model for Fair Multitarget Tracking in Cooperative Multirobot Systems
description: A swarm of autonomous mobile robots equipped with short range sensors keeps track of a (possibly larger) number of mobile targets. We propose a model for CMOMMT scenarios based on an integer linear formulation and Bayesian networks.

title: Learning Ground Traversability from Simulations

title: Image Classification for Ground Traversability Estimation in Robotics
booktitle: Advanced Concepts for Intelligent Vision Systems
description: We address traversability estimation as a heightmap classification problem: we build a convolutional neural network that, given an image representing a patch of terrain, predicts whether the robot will be able to traverse it.

title: A Machine Learning Approach to Visual Perception of Forest Trails for Mobile Robots
